title: dactylorhiza maculata var. rhoumenis
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata var. rhoumenis, also known as the spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the cardiovascular system, lower blood pressure, and improve circulation. The spotted marsh orchid has been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as hypertension, angina, and Raynaud's disease, with its health benefits attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
